heading: Reimbursement for transfer of excess property
label: 522 Reimbursement for transfer of excess property
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)52240:483(a)(1) (last sentence)., title II, § 202(a)(1) (last sentence), ; , .June 30, 1949, ch. 28863 Stat. 384July 12, 1952, ch. 703, § 1(f)66 Stat. 593In subsection (a), the words “Director of the Office of Management and Budget” are substituted for “Director of the Bureau of the Budget” in section 202(a)(1) (last sentence) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 because the office of Director of the Bureau of the Budget was redesignated the Director of the Office of Management and Budget by section 102(b) of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1970 (eff. , ). Section 102 of Reorganization Plan No. 2 of 1970, was repealed by (, ), the first section of which enacted Title 31, United States Code, but the successor provision, 31:502, continued the designation as Director of the Office of Management and Budget.July 1, 197084 Stat. 2085section 5(b) of the Act of September 13, 1982Public Law 97–25896 Stat. 1085In subsection (b)(1), the reference to “section 204(b)” in section 202(a)(1) (last sentence) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 is translated as a reference to section 204(c) of the Act because subsection (b) was redesignated as (c) by the Act of (ch.1178, ).August 31, 195468 Stat. 1051In subsection (b)(2)(A), the words “chapter 91 of title 31” are substituted for “the Government Corporation Control Act (, )” in section 202(a)(1) (last sentence) of the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act of 1949 because of (, ), the first section of which enacted Title 31, United States Code.59 Stat. 59731 U.S.C. 841section 4(b) of the Act of September 13, 1982Public Law 97–25896 Stat. 1067In subsection (c), the word “at” is substituted for “as” (in the phrase “as [sic] prices set by the Administrator”) to reflect the probable intent of Congress. See Senate Report No. 2075, dated (United States Code Congressional and Administrative News, 82nd Congress, 2d Session, 1952, Volume 2, p. 2123).July 2, 1952
Amendments2006—Subsec. (a). , struck out “of this section” after “subsections (b) and (c)”.Pub. L. 109–284, § 6(1)Subsec. (b). , substituted “at” for “At” in heading.Pub. L. 109–284, § 6(2)

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: Subject to subsections (b) and (c), the Administrator of General Services, with the approval of the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, shall prescribe the amount of reimbursement required for a transfer of excess property.
net proceeds are requested under ; orsection 574(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: subject to chapter 91 of title 31; or

children.label: B
children.text: an organization specified in .section 321(c)(2) of this title

children.heading: .—Distribution Through General Services Administration Supply Centers
children.label: c .—Distribution Through General Services Administration Supply Centers
children.text: Excess property determined by the Administrator to be suitable for distribution through the supply centers of the General Services Administration shall be retransferred at prices set by the Administrator with due regard to prices established under .section 321(d) of this title
